{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks about the location of the epidural venous plexus. From the retrieved documents, particularly Document [3], Document [5], and Document [19], it is clearly stated that the epidural venous plexus (also known as the Batson plexus) is located in the epidural space within the vertebral canal, specifically in the extradural space surrounding the spinal cord. Document [3] states: 'The internal vertebral venous plexus is made up of two anterior longitudinal veins coursing on either side of the posterior longitudinal ligament and two posterior veins coursing anterior to the ligamentum flavum.' These veins are located in the epidural space, which is below the dura mater. Document [5] confirms: 'The spinal cord drains via the single anterior spinal vein and single posterior spinal vein which in turn drain into the internal vertebral venous plexus located in the epidural space within the extradural fat.' This confirms the location is in the vertebral canal, below the dura mater. Option C states 'In vertebral canal below duramater,' which matches this anatomical description. Options A, B, and D refer to locations in the cranial fossa or above the dura, which are not relevant to the epidural venous plexus in the spinal canal. Therefore, the correct answer is C.", "answer_choice": "C"}
